api: BoldSign API v1 (https://api.boldsign.com), OpenAPI 3.0.4 spec at https://api.boldsign.com/swagger/v1/swagger.json
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every one of the 85 documented BoldSign v1 endpoints is enumerated below, each covered_by a stream/write or excluded with a specific reason. 8 read streams (documents, templates, teams, contacts, brands, users, contact_groups, sender_identities) and 8 write actions (2 team, 2 contact, 3 contact-group, 6 document-lifecycle, 2 user-lifecycle) are implemented. Document/template SENDING (multipart file upload + recipient/field placement) and embedded-URL generation are out of scope: BoldSign's core e-signature send/create/embed flows are not safe generic reverse-ETL targets and legacy itself never implemented them (capabilities.write was false pre-expansion).

excluded.reason: single-brand detail GET redundant with the brand_id-keyed brands list stream already covered; no per-brand data unavailable from /v1/brand/list
brand creation carries embedded logo/asset upload concerns beyond a generic reverse-ETL create action; deferred, not a safe generic write target for this wave
brand edit shares brand/create's asset-upload-adjacent surface; deferred alongside it
irreversible deletion of an account-wide branding profile; not a safe generic write target
account-wide default-brand toggle, not a record-shaped mutation
single-group detail GET redundant with the contact_groups list stream already covered
single-contact detail GET redundant with the contacts list stream already covered

BoldSign's real wire request body for contact creation is a JSON ARRAY of ContactDetails objects ([{...}]), not a single JSON object; the engine's write dialect only ever sends one JSON object (or form) per record, with no array-wrapping body option (write.go's executeWriteRecord). Modeling this write would either send a malformed single-object body BoldSign's API would reject, or silently wrap it in a way the dialect cannot express declaratively. ENGINE_GAP: no array-body write shape exists in writes.json today.

custom fields are a brand-scoped template-authoring concern (form-field definitions for the Designer UI), not an object catalog stream comparable to documents/templates/contacts; deferred to a future wave
deferred alongside customField/list; template-authoring surface, not a reverse-ETL target
deferred alongside customField/list
deferred alongside customField/list
generates an embedded-Designer session URL for interactive UI use, not a syncable object or data mutation
per-signer access-code authentication management is a fine-grained signing-flow control, not a generic reverse-ETL action; deferred
deferred alongside RemoveAuthentication
deferred alongside RemoveAuthentication
mid-flow recipient re-assignment is a signing-flow control with cascading email/notification side effects beyond a simple field update; deferred
an on-behalf-of-scoped variant of the same document listing already covered by the documents stream; the on_behalf_of scoping parameter is not modeled as a separate stream
cancels an in-progress interactive document-edit session; no meaningful use outside the embedded-editor UI flow
generates an embedded-editor session URL for interactive UI use, not a syncable object or mutation
generates an embedded-send session URL for interactive UI use, not a data mutation
returns a raw PDF byte stream, not a JSON record; no declarative binary-download stream shape exists in this dialect
returns a raw file byte stream, same as document/download
returns a raw PDF audit-trail byte stream
sends a previously-drafted document for signature; part of the multipart create/send flow this wave defers entirely
full document re-edit (recipients/fields/files) is a multipart, form-placement-heavy operation matching document/send's deferred scope
narrow single-field lifecycle tweak judged lower-value than the 6 document actions already covered; candidate for a future increment
generates an embedded-signing session URL for interactive UI use, not a syncable object
form-field prefill is a signing-flow authoring concern tightly coupled to a document's field layout, not a generic record mutation
single-document detail GET redundant with the documents list stream (which already emits every field this endpoint returns) already covered
a team-scoped variant of the same document listing already covered by the documents stream

multipart file-upload + recipient/field-placement document send; the core e-signature request flow is not a safe generic reverse-ETL write target and legacy itself never implemented it (capabilities.write was false pre-expansion)

account-level API-credit balance counter, not a syncable object catalog

BoldSign's real wire request body for user creation is a JSON ARRAY of CreateUser objects ([{...}]), the identical array-body shape contacts/create has — the engine's write dialect has no array-wrapping body option (see contacts/create's excluded entry). ENGINE_GAP: no array-body write shape exists in writes.json today.
narrow invitation-lifecycle action judged lower-value than update_user/change_user_team already covered; candidate for a future increment

arbitrary key-value metadata blob update, judged lower-value than the role/team/status fields already covered by update_user/change_user_team
